intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of advanced degradation. The expected registry (P1-P4) is largely absent or non-viable. Only the P3/P4 container shows any structural remnants, though these are likely necrotic.

inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant material detected in the black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: The Pothos is absent; only debris remains.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: The Silver Guest is absent; only debris remains.

plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface. No signs of Mexican Mint.

plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface. No signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, damp, and undisturbed across all containers.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (possibly crushed eggshells or mineral deposits) on the surface of the black pot.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no organic matter or spillages observed.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: The white cylindrical object was introduced near the yellow pot. The soil surface remained static with no signs of germination or recovery.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: No observable change in the biological state; the biome remains devoid of living plant specimens.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-functional. All registered specimens have suffered total systemic loss. The environment is currently a 'dead' soil state.
anomalies: Introduction of a white cylindrical object (bottle) near the yellow pot.
Total absence of the white rabbit scale anchor.
Presence of white granular debris in the black pot.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit confirms a complete failure of the indoor biome. Across the five-day observation window, there has been no evidence of plant life. The pots contain only soil and some unidentified granular debris. The white rabbit anchor, previously associated with P3, is missing. The introduction of a foreign object (the bottle) suggests the desk is being used for storage rather than cultivation.
